etymology.description: 問(問) (wèn, "ask") + 路(路) (lù, "road; path") = to ask for directions
etymology.interpretation: In Chinese, 问(問) (wèn, "ask") refers to the action of posing a question, while 路(路) (lù, "road; path") refers to the physical pathways one travels on. Combining these characters, 问路(問路) (wèn lù) means to seek guidance or directions regarding the right path to take.

examples.chinese: 他向别人问路(他向別人問路)。
在陌生城市迷路时(在陌生城市迷路時)，他向路人问路(他向路人問路)。
examples.pinyin: Zài mòshēng chéngshì mílù shí, tā xiàng lùrén wèn lù.
examples.english: When lost in an unfamiliar city, he asks passersby for directions.

examples.chinese: 出门前(出門前)，他会先问路(他會先問路)。
examples.pinyin: Chūmén qián, tā huì xiān wèn lù.
examples.english: Before leaving home, he asks for directions first.
